name: punjabi
description: Butter-heavy tandoor-centric. Dal makhani, butter chicken, sarson ka saag, chole bhature.

name: kashmiri
description: Wazwan tradition. Rogan josh, dum aloo, yakhni. Fennel, dry ginger, saffron.

name: rajasthani
description: Desert cuisine. Dal baati churma, gatte ki sabzi, laal maas. Heavy ghee.

name: himachali
description: Mountain cuisine. Dham thali, madra, siddu. Yogurt-based gravies.

name: uttarakhandi
description: Garhwali + Kumaoni. Kafuli, chainsoo, bhatt ki churkani. Lentil-heavy.

name: delhi
description: Melting pot. Chole bhature, paranthas, chaat, kebabs. Punjabi + Mughlai + UP influences.

name: up
description: Broader UP home cooking. Tahiri, bedmi puri, baati chokha, kachori-sabzi.

name: bihari
description: Litti chokha, sattu paratha, thekua, dal pitha. Sattu-centric, mustard oil.

name: bundelkhandi
description: Rustic. Bafla, maheri, lapsi.

name: sindhi
description: Dal pakwan, sindhi kadhi, sai bhaji, koki. Gram flour heavy, tamarind-forward.

name: tamil
description: Rice-centric, tamarind-forward. Sambar, rasam, poriyal, kootu.

name: chettinad
description: Fiery merchant cuisine. Chettinad chicken, kuzhambu. Unique spices.

name: kerala
description: Coconut-everything. Appam-stew, fish molee, avial, puttu-kadala, sadya.

name: malabar
description: Muslim-influenced Kerala. Malabar biryani, pathiri.

name: andhra
description: Chilli-forward. Gongura, pesarattu, pulihora. India's spiciest mainstream cuisine.

name: telangana
description: Sakinalu, jonna rotte, sarva pindi. More millet-based than coastal Andhra.

name: karnataka
description: Bisi bele bath, ragi mudde, jolada rotti. Diverse by sub-region.

name: udupi
description: Temple cuisine. Masala dosa, set dosa, sambar. Strictly vegetarian.

name: mangalorean
description: Coastal. Kori rotti, fish gassi, neer dosa. Seafood-heavy.

name: coorg
description: Kodava tribal. Pandi curry (pork), bamboo shoot curry.

name: bengali
description: Fish and rice culture. Machher jhol, shorshe ilish, aloo posto, sandesh.

name: odia
description: Temple-influenced. Dalma, pakhala bhaat, chhena poda. Light, less oily.

name: assamese
description: Khar, masor tenga, aloo pitika. Minimal oil, fermented ingredients, bhut jolokia.

name: jharkhandi
description: Tribal/Adivasi. Dhuska, rugra. Forest ingredients, rustic.

name: maharashtrian
description: Vada pav, misal pav, puran poli, thalipeeth. Peanut + coconut base.

name: malvani
description: Coastal. Malvani fish curry, sol kadhi. Coconut-heavy, fiery.

name: kolhapuri
description: Extreme heat. Tambda/pandhra rassa. Special Kolhapuri masala.

name: vidarbha
description: Saoji cuisine — extremely spicy. More Central Indian influence.

name: gujarati
description: Sweet-in-everything. Dhokla, thepla, undhiyu, khandvi. Sugar/jaggery in curries.

name: kutchi
description: Desert Gujarati. Dabeli, bajri rotla. Millet-heavy.

name: goan
description: Portuguese-influenced. Vindaloo, xacuti, bebinca. Vinegar, kokum, coconut.

name: konkani
description: Broader coastal. Sol kadhi, amboli. Coconut + kokum + seafood.

name: parsi
description: Zoroastrian community. Dhansak, patra ni machhi, salli boti. Sweet-sour-spicy.

name: madhya_pradesh
description: Bhopali + Indori + Malwa. Varies widely by city.

name: indori
description: Street food powerhouse. Poha-jalebi, bhutte ka kees. Snacking capital.

name: chhattisgarhi
description: Rice-and-lentil tribal. Fara, muthiya, bore baasi. Simple, minimal spice.

name: naga
description: Fermented, smoked, fiery. Smoked pork, axone, raja mirchi chutney.

name: manipuri
description: Eromba, singju, kangshoi. Fermented fish, light, soup-heavy.

name: mizo
description: Bai, sawhchiar. Boiled/steamed, closest to Southeast Asian in India.

name: sikkimese
description: Tibetan-Nepali influenced. Momos, thukpa, gundruk, sel roti.

name: meghalayan
description: Jadoh, tungrymbai. Khasi + Garo tribal. Black sesame.

name: arunachali
description: Thukpa, pika pila. Minimal spice, smoked meats.

name: tripuri
description: Mui borok, berma. Bengali-influenced with tribal preparations.

name: mughlai
description: Rich Mughal court cuisine. Biryanis, kormas, kebabs, nihari. Cream, nuts, saffron.

name: awadhi
description: Refined Nawabi. Dum cooking, galouti kebab, Lucknowi biryani.

name: hyderabadi
description: Nizam court cuisine. Hyderabadi biryani, haleem, mirchi ka salan.

name: street_food_generic
description: Chaat, vada pav, pav bhaji, pani puri, bhel puri, kathi roll.

name: indian_bakery
description: Pav, khari biscuit, maska bun, nankhatai, plum cake.

name: indo_chinese
description: Hakka noodles, manchurian, chilli chicken/paneer, fried rice.

name: chinese_authentic
description: Dim sum, mapo tofu, Sichuan. Growing in metros.

name: indian_tibetan
description: Momos (Indian style), thukpa, tingmo. Tibetan base + Indian spices.

name: continental
description: Indian catch-all for Western food. Grilled chicken, soups, salads, sandwiches.

name: italian
description: Pasta, pizza, risotto. Widely eaten in urban India.

name: american
description: Burgers, fries, mac and cheese, BBQ, pancakes.

name: mediterranean
description: Grain bowls, grilled meats, olive oil based. Health-food trend.

name: anglo_indian
description: Mulligatawny soup, railway mutton curry, jhal frezi. British-Indian colonial fusion.

name: thai
description: Pad thai, green/red curry, tom yum. Coconut base familiar to South Indians.

name: japanese
description: Sushi, ramen, tempura, teriyaki. Niche but growing.

name: korean
description: Kimchi, bibimbap, Korean fried chicken. K-wave driving adoption in Gen Z.

name: middle_eastern_generic
description: Hummus, falafel, shawarma, kebab platters.

name: lebanese
description: Tabbouleh, manoushe, kibbeh. Often listed separately on Indian menus.

name: bhutanese
description: Ema datshi, phaksha paa. Chilli + cheese focused.

name: burmese
description: Mohinga, khow suey, tea leaf salad. SE Asian + Indian fusion.

name: mexican
description: Tacos, burritos, quesadillas. Corn + beans + chilli.

name: vietnamese
description: Pho, banh mi, spring rolls. Fresh herbs + light broths.
